properties.description: The Microsoft cloud security benchmark initiative represents the policies and controls implementing security recommendations defined in Microsoft cloud security benchmark, see https://aka.ms/MCSB. This also serves as the Microsoft Defender for Cloud default policy initiative. You can directly assign this initiative, or manage its policies and compliance results within Microsoft Defender for Cloud.

metadata.displayName: Shared: Log Analytics workspace resource ID
metadata.description: Single Log Analytics workspace used by all diagnostic-settings policies in this initiative. Provide the full resource ID of a target workspace. If empty, the diagnostic-setting Log Analytics policies will not produce settings.

metadata.displayName: Shared: Event Hub authorization rule resource ID
metadata.description: Single Event Hub namespace authorization-rule resource ID used by all diagnostic-settings policies in this initiative. If empty, the diagnostic-setting Event Hub policies will not produce settings.

metadata.displayName: Shared: Event Hub region
metadata.description: Azure region of the shared Event Hub namespace. Diagnostic-settings policies only target resources in this region. Leave empty to opt out of Event Hub diagnostics.

metadata.displayName: Shared: Storage account resource ID
metadata.description: Single storage account resource ID used by all diagnostic-settings policies in this initiative. If empty, the diagnostic-setting Storage policies will not produce settings.

metadata.displayName: Shared: Storage account region
metadata.description: Azure region of the shared storage account. Diagnostic-settings policies only target resources in this region. Leave empty to opt out of Storage diagnostics.

metadata.displayName: Manage certificate validity period
metadata.description: Enable or disable manage certificate validity period.

metadata.displayName: The maximum validity period in months of managed certificate
metadata.description: The limit to how long a certificate may be valid for. Certificates with lengthy validity periods aren't best practice.

metadata.displayName: Key Vault secrets should have expiration dates set
metadata.description: Enable or disable key vault secrets should have expiration dates set.

metadata.displayName: Key Vault keys should have expiration dates set
metadata.description: Enable or disable key vault keys should have expiration dates set.

metadata.displayName: Audit unrestricted network access to storage accounts
metadata.description: Enable or disable the monitoring of network access to storage account

metadata.displayName: Network Security Groups on the subnet level should be enabled
metadata.description: Enable or disable monitoring of NSGs on subnets

metadata.displayName: SQL Managed Instance TDE protector should be encrypted with your own key
metadata.description: Enable or disable the monitoring of Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) with your own key support. TDE with your own key support provides increased transparency and control over the TDE Protector, increased security with an HSM-backed external service, and promotion of separation of duties.

metadata.displayName: Arc Windows Monitoring Effect
metadata.description: Enable or disable monitoring of Arc connected Windows machines

metadata.displayName: Cognitive Services accounts should enable data encryption with a customer-managed key
metadata.description: Customer-managed keys are commonly required to meet regulatory compliance standards. Customer-managed keys enable the data stored in Cognitive Services to be encrypted with an Azure Key Vault key created and owned by you. You have full control and responsibility for the key lifecycle, including rotation and management. Learn more about customer-managed key encryption at https://aka.ms/cosmosdb-cmk.

metadata.displayName: Azure Cosmos DB accounts should use customer-managed keys to encrypt data at rest
metadata.description: Use customer-managed keys to manage the encryption at rest of your Azure Cosmos DB. By default, the data is encrypted at rest with service-managed keys, but customer-managed keys are commonly required to meet regulatory compliance standards. Customer-managed keys enable the data to be encrypted with an Azure Key Vault key created and owned by you. You have full control and responsibility for the key lifecycle, including rotation and management. Learn more about customer-managed key encryption at https://aka.ms/cosmosdb-cmk.

metadata.displayName: Storage accounts should use customer-managed key for encryption
metadata.description: Secure your storage account with greater flexibility using customer-managed keys. When you specify a customer-managed key, that key is used to protect and control access to the key that encrypts your data. Using customer-managed keys provides additional capabilities to control rotation of the key encryption key or cryptographically erase data.

metadata.displayName: Container registries should be encrypted with a customer-managed key
metadata.description: Use customer-managed keys to manage the encryption at rest of the contents of your registries. By default, the data is encrypted at rest with service-managed keys, but customer-managed keys are commonly required to meet regulatory compliance standards. Customer-managed keys enable the data to be encrypted with an Azure Key Vault key created and owned by you. You have full control and responsibility for the key lifecycle, including rotation and management. Learn more about customer-managed key encryption at https://aka.ms/acr/CMK.

metadata.displayName: Azure Machine Learning workspaces should be encrypted with a customer-managed key
metadata.description: Manage encryption at rest of your Azure Machine Learning workspace data with customer-managed keys. By default, customer data is encrypted with service-managed keys, but customer-managed keys are commonly required to meet regulatory compliance standards. Customer-managed keys enable the data to be encrypted with an Azure Key Vault key created and owned by you. You have full control and responsibility for the key lifecycle, including rotation and management. Learn more about customer-managed key encryption at https://aka.ms/azureml-workspaces-cmk.

metadata.displayName: SQL server TDE protector should be encrypted with your own key
metadata.description: Enable or disable the monitoring of Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) with your own key support. TDE with your own key support provides increased transparency and control over the TDE Protector, increased security with an HSM-backed external service, and promotion of separation of duties.

metadata.displayName: Data Protection (BYOK) should be enabled for MySQL servers
metadata.description: Enable or disable the monitoring of Data Protection with Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) support

metadata.displayName: Data Protection (BYOK) should be enabled for PostgreSQL servers
metadata.description: Enable or disable the monitoring of Data Protection with Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) support

metadata.displayName: Private endpoint should be configured for Key Vault
metadata.description: Private endpoints connect your virtual network to Azure services without a public IP address at the source or destination. By mapping private endpoints to key vault, you can reduce data leakage risks.

metadata.displayName: Keys: the maximum validity period in days
metadata.description: Specify the maximum number of days a key can be valid for. Keys should be ephemeral. Using a key with a long validity period is not recommended.

metadata.displayName: Keys: the maximum validity period in days after activation
metadata.description: Specify the maximum number of days a key can be valid for after activation.

metadata.displayName: Secrets: the minimum days before expiration
metadata.description: Specify the minimum number of days that a secret should remain usable prior to expiration.

metadata.displayName: Secrets: the maximum validity period in days
metadata.description: Specify the maximum number of days a secret can be valid for. Secrets should be ephemeral. Using a secret with a long validity period is not recommended.

metadata.displayName: Secrets: the maximum validity period in days after activation
metadata.description: Specify the maximum number of days a secret can be valid for after activation.

metadata.displayName: Certificates: days to expire
metadata.description: The number of days for a certificate to expire.

metadata.displayName: Managed Identity Federated Credentials from Azure Kubernetes: allowed tenants
metadata.description: The list of allowed Azure AD tenant ID's of AKS OIDC issuers. Empty to allow all tenants.

metadata.displayName: Managed Identity Federated Credentials from GitHub: allowed repository owners
metadata.description: The list of allowed owners. Empty to allow all owners.

metadata.displayName: Kubernetes cluster containers: allowed registry or registries regex
metadata.description: The RegEx rule used to match allowed container image field in a Kubernetes cluster. For example, to allow any Azure Container Registry image by matching partial path: ^[^\/]+\.azurecr\.io\/.+$ and for multiple registries: ^([^\/]+\.azurecr\.io|registry\.io)\/.+$

metadata.displayName: Kubernetes cluster services: allowed service ports list
metadata.description: The list of service ports allowed in a Kubernetes cluster. Array only accepts strings. Example: ["443", "80"]

metadata.displayName: Kubernetes cluster containers: max allowed CPU units
metadata.description: The maximum CPU units allowed for a container. E.g. 200m. For more information, please refer https://aka.ms/k8s-policy-pod-limits

metadata.displayName: Kubernetes cluster containers: max allowed memory bytes
metadata.description: The maximum memory bytes allowed for a container. E.g. 1Gi. For more information, please refer https://aka.ms/k8s-policy-pod-limits

metadata.displayName: Configure backup on virtual machines without a given tag to an existing recovery services vault in the same location : Location (Specify the location of the VMs that you want to protect)
metadata.description: Specify the location of the VMs that you want to protect. VMs should be backed up to a vault in the same location. For example - southeastasia.

metadata.displayName: Configure backup on virtual machines without a given tag to an existing recovery services vault in the same location : Backup Policy (of type Azure VM from a vault in the location chosen above)
metadata.description: Specify the id of the Azure backup policy to configure backup of the virtual machines. The selected Azure backup policy should be of type Azure virtual machine. This policy needs to be in a vault that is present in the location chosen above. For example - /subscriptions/<SubscriptionId>/resourceGroups/<resourceGroupName>/providers/Microsoft.RecoveryServices/vaults/<VaultName>/backupPolicies/<BackupPolicyName>.

metadata.displayName: Completion content filter name
metadata.description: Cognitive Services Deployments should only use allowed completion content filtering : Content Filter

metadata.displayName: Multi-severity content filter name
metadata.description: Cognitive Services Deployments should only use allowed control : Content Filter

metadata.displayName: Prompt content filter name
metadata.description: Cognitive Services Deployments should only use allowed prompt content filtering : Content Filter

metadata.displayName: The list of approved extension types that can be installed. Example: AzureDiskEncryption
metadata.description: Only approved VM extensions should be installed : Approved extensions

metadata.displayName: The common name (CN) of the Certificate Authority (CA) provider. For example, for an issuer CN = Contoso, OU = .., DC = .., you can specify Contoso
metadata.description: Certificates should be issued by the specified non-integrated certificate authority : The common name of the certificate authority.

metadata.displayName: Certificates should have the specified lifetime action triggers : The maximum lifetime percentage
metadata.description: Enter the percentage of lifetime of the certificate when you want to trigger the policy action. For example, to trigger a policy action at 80% of the certificate's valid life, enter '80'.

metadata.displayName: Certificates should have the specified lifetime action triggers : The minimum days before expiry
metadata.description: Enter the days before expiration of the certificate when you want to trigger the policy action. For example, to trigger a policy action 90 days before the certificate's expiration, enter '90'.

metadata.displayName: The minimum key size for RSA certificates
metadata.description: Certificates using RSA cryptography should have the specified minimum key size : Minimum RSA key size

metadata.displayName: The minimum number of days that a key should remain usable prior to expiration
metadata.description: Keys should have more than the specified number of days before expiration : The minimum days before expiration

metadata.displayName: The minimum key size for RSA keys
metadata.description: Keys using RSA cryptography should have a specified minimum key size : Minimum RSA key size

metadata.displayName: [Preview]: Kubernetes cluster containers should use only allowed sysctl interfaces : Allowed sysctls
metadata.description: The list of plain sysctl names or sysctl patterns which end with *. The string * matches all sysctls. For more information, visit https://aka.ms/k8s-policy-sysctl-interfaces.

metadata.displayName: Prints a message if a mutation is applied : Map of mutations annotations and respective messages
metadata.description: The annotations are mapped to respective messages that will be printed upon resource mutation

metadata.displayName: [Preview]: Assign Built-In User-Assigned Managed Identity to Virtual Machine Scale Sets : Bring Your Own User-Assigned Identity
metadata.description: Enable this to use your pre-created user-assigned managed identity. The pre-created identity MUST exist within the subscription otherwise the policy deployment will fail. If enabled, ensure that the User-Assigned Identity Name and Identity Resource Group Name parameters match the pre-created identity. If not enabled, the policy will create per subscription, per resource user-assigned managed identities in a new resource group named 'Built-In-Identity-RG'.

metadata.displayName: [Preview]: Assign Built-In User-Assigned Managed Identity to Virtual Machines : Bring Your Own User-Assigned Identity
metadata.description: Enable this to use your pre-created user-assigned managed identity. The pre-created identity MUST exist within the subscription otherwise the policy deployment will fail. If enabled, ensure that the User-Assigned Identity Name and Identity Resource Group Name parameters match the pre-created identity. If not enabled, the policy will create per subscription, per resource user-assigned managed identities in a new resource group named 'Built-In-Identity-RG'.

metadata.displayName: An activity log alert should exist for specific Administrative operations : Operation Name
metadata.description: Administrative Operation name for which activity log alert should be configured

metadata.displayName: An activity log alert should exist for specific Policy operations : Operation Name
metadata.description: Policy Operation name for which activity log alert should exist

metadata.displayName: An activity log alert should exist for specific Security operations : Operation Name
metadata.description: Security Operation name for which activity log alert should exist

metadata.displayName: Public IP addresses should have resource logs enabled for Azure DDoS Protection : Diagnostic setting name
metadata.description: Profile name for the Azure diagnostic settings resource

metadata.displayName: Public IP addresses should have resource logs enabled for Azure DDoS Protection : Log Analytics workspace
metadata.description: The target Log Analytics workspace for the diagnostic settings

metadata.displayName: A custom IPsec/IKE policy must be applied to all Azure virtual network gateway connections : IPsec Encryption
metadata.description: IPsec Encryption

metadata.displayName: A custom IPsec/IKE policy must be applied to all Azure virtual network gateway connections : IPsec Integrity
metadata.description: IPsec Integrity

metadata.displayName: A custom IPsec/IKE policy must be applied to all Azure virtual network gateway connections : IKE Encryption
metadata.description: IKE Encryption

metadata.displayName: A custom IPsec/IKE policy must be applied to all Azure virtual network gateway connections : IKE Integrity
metadata.description: IKE Integrity

metadata.displayName: A custom IPsec/IKE policy must be applied to all Azure virtual network gateway connections : DH Group
metadata.description: DH Group

metadata.displayName: A custom IPsec/IKE policy must be applied to all Azure virtual network gateway connections : PFS Group
metadata.description: PFS Group

metadata.displayName: Virtual machines should be connected to an approved virtual network : Virtual network Id
metadata.description: Resource Id of the virtual network. Example: /subscriptions/YourSubscriptionId/resourceGroups/YourResourceGroupName/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/Name

metadata.displayName: Virtual networks should use specified virtual network gateway : Virtual network gateway Id
metadata.description: Resource Id of the virtual network gateway. Example: /subscriptions/YourSubscriptionId/resourceGroups/YourResourceGroup/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways/Name
